is a historic house in [Hibbing, Minnesota](/wiki/Hibbing,_Minnesota "Hibbing, Minnesota"), United States. It was built in 1897 as a residence for executives of the Oliver Mining Company.[[2]](#cite_note-History-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Skrief-3) The house was listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1980 for its local significance in the themes of architecture and social history.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It was nominated for reflecting the sumptuous lifestyle enjoyed by an elite few in the early mining era.[[3]](#cite_note-Skrief-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Historic house in Minnesota, United States

United States historic place

| Location | 2145 4th Avenue, [Hibbing, Minnesota](/wiki/Hibbing,_Minnesota "Hibbing, Minnesota") |
| Coordinates | [47°25′30.5″N 92°56′17.5″W﻿ / ﻿47.425139°N 92.938194°W﻿ / 47.425139; -92.938194](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mitchell%E2%80%93Tappan_House&params=47_25_30.5_N_92_56_17.5_W_type:landmark_region:US-MN) |
| Area | Less than one acre |
| Built | 1897 |
| Architectural style | [Queen Anne](/wiki/Queen_Anne_style_architecture_in_the_United_States "Queen Anne style architecture in the United States") |
| NRHP reference No. | [80004352](https://npgallery.nps.gov/AssetDetail/NRIS/80004352)[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) |
| Added to NRHP | December 2, 1980 |

The **Mitchell–Tappan House** is a historic house in [Hibbing, Minnesota](/wiki/Hibbing,_Minnesota "Hibbing, Minnesota"), United States. It was built in 1897 as a residence for executives of the Oliver Mining Company.[[2]](#cite_note-History-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Skrief-3) The house was listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1980 for its local significance in the themes of architecture and social history.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It was nominated for reflecting the sumptuous lifestyle enjoyed by an elite few in the early mining era.[[3]](#cite_note-Skrief-3)

The Oliver Mining Company continued to use the Mitchell–Tappan House as an executive residence into the 1960s.[[3]](#cite_note-Skrief-3) In recent years its owners operated it as a [bed and breakfast](/wiki/Bed_and_breakfast "Bed and breakfast").[[2]](#cite_note-History-2)
